Executive Profile

The hard part is changing work without breaking trust.

Chandra Kanojia works where AI, data, CRM/Salesforce, workflow, analytics, and enterprise platforms have to become real operating change inside regulated institutions.

His career arc spans global consulting and Fortune 100 buyer-side execution. The common thread is practical: make the system of work clearer, faster, safer, and easier to run.

Distinction

Pattern recognition from consulting. Operating judgment from the buyer side. A clear view of how data, workflow, controls, and platforms have to line up before AI can matter.

Operating proof spans

Regulated financial services · data readiness for AI · Salesforce/CRM modernization · workflow and case management · analytics and governance · service and advisor platforms · platform reliability · global consulting mandates · insurance, retirement, brokerage, healthcare, and claims transformation.

Mandates Chandra is credible to lead

Where the profile becomes useful.

01

Make AI useful beyond the demo

Move AI into real workflows with clear ownership, evidence, controls, adoption paths, and business accountability.

02

Modernize CRM and workflow systems

Improve the systems where work moves: CRM, case management, service platforms, advisor enablement, analytics, and enterprise workflow.

03

Make data usable at the decision point

Close the gap between data that exists somewhere and data that is trusted when people, systems, and AI need to act.

Career arc

Consulting pattern recognition. Operator-side execution.

Fidelity Investments

Buyer-side execution across AI-enabled workflow, Salesforce/CRM, analytics, service platforms, governance, advisor and associate enablement, data context, and platform reliability.

Accenture

Strategy and transformation advisory across customer, sales, service, CRM, operating model, and value-realization work.

Cognizant

North America financial-services digital consulting leadership, CXO relationships, advisory demand shaping, and transformation-market credibility.

Virtusa and early Cognizant consulting

Global insurance, healthcare, underwriting, claims, legal, and financial-services transformation across the US, UK, Germany, and South Asia.

Education & Development

Business strategy, technology governance, and AI product judgment.

Education: IIM Bangalore, MBA; Carnegie Mellon University, CIO Program; NIT Allahabad, B.Tech.

Executive development: BCG Digital Leader; AI product strategy; product management; enterprise architecture; Salesforce/CRM exposure.

Additional delivery credentials and technical training are available in the executive profile shared for relevant conversations.

Discuss the mandate

Start with the operating problem.

Relevant conversations include AI execution, data readiness, CRM/Salesforce and workflow modernization, platform reliability, governance, adoption, and measurable business value.
